claim: Agents fail in production due to brittle workflows, lack of contextual learning, and inability to improve over time; trace-based testing and observability are critical for moving agents from demos to production
actionable: Implement observability and tracing from the start of agent development; use trace analysis to identify edge cases and iteratively improve reliability
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Pratik Verma: Observability Agent to Find & Fix Issues in AI Agents

claim: Agents usually fail not in code logic but in edge cases, missing context, and missing tools when deployed with real-world ambiguous inputs
actionable: Test agents with diverse, ambiguous, real-world inputs early; don't assume happy-path logic is sufficient for production reliability
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Pratik Verma: Observability Agent to Find & Fix Issues in AI Agents

claim: The recommended loop: deploy agents in real-world simulations, observe with tracing, run evaluation/testing to identify issues, pass findings to coding agents to fix, then deploy and iterate until acceptable reliability
actionable: Implement the deploy-observe-evaluate-fix loop as your standard agent development cycle; automate the integration with coding agents
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Pratik Verma: Observability Agent to Find & Fix Issues in AI Agents

claim: Project Monocle provides framework-agnostic agentic tracing; instrumenting telemetry properly upfront prevents wasteful post-hoc data cleansing
actionable: Use Monocle or similar tracing frameworks to instrument agent workflows before deployment; design telemetry structure upfront rather than cleaning it after the fact
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Pratik Verma: Observability Agent to Find & Fix Issues in AI Agents

claim: Test-driven loops with evaluation frameworks allow detection of silent failures (logic issues with no exceptions) alongside error-based failures, improving overall agent quality
actionable: Define evaluation criteria beyond exception detection; catch logic failures and quality issues through scored evaluations in your testing framework
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Pratik Verma: Observability Agent to Find & Fix Issues in AI Agents

claim: Observability agents enriched with traces and knowledge graphs can answer detailed diagnostic questions about agent failures (timing, related failures, affected components) enabling proper context handoff to coding agents
actionable: Build or integrate an observability agent that can query trace data and failures; use this to generate enriched context for coding agents to fix issues
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Pratik Verma: Observability Agent to Find & Fix Issues in AI Agents
